extra.reasoning: The mutual-exclusion property requires that no input makes both rec_drug_a and rec_drug_b true. rec_drug_a is set true only by r5 (priority 50): IF (immunosuppressed AND asthma). rec_drug_b is set true only by r2 (priority 50): IF immunosuppressed. No rule ever sets rec_drug_a or rec_drug_b to false, and defaults only apply to outputs left unset. With immunosuppressed=true and asthma=true, both r5 and r2 fire, producing rec_drug_a=true and rec_drug_b=true simultaneously. No higher-priority or competing rule overrides either. Thus the property is violated. (rec_drug_c stays false here since diabetes/prior_gi_bleed/ckd_dialysis are all false, but that is irrelevant to the a-vs-b conflict.)
